title: Title
featured_image_hint: Enter a relative path (/uploads/images/...) or a full URL.
author: Author
translation_save_first: Save the post first to add its translations.
translation_status_follow_source: This translation follows the publication state of the source post.
frontend_translation_fallback_notice: This article is not yet available in :requested_locale. Showing the :source_locale version.
confirm_delete_post: Are you sure you want to delete this post?
home_posts_bridge_text: The blog helps explain the FlatCMS spirit before you move on to downloads or your first deployment.
continue_reading_subtitle: Three close reads to keep exploring the topic.
menu_custom_label_warning: Some menu items use a custom label for this post. Update them to avoid inconsistencies.
confirm_delete_posts_batch: Are you sure you want to permanently delete the selected posts?
posts_tour_list_toolbar_content: Filter posts by category, create a new article, and open the trash from this toolbar.
posts_tour_list_batch_content: Select several articles and archive or delete them in bulk without handling rows one by one.
posts_tour_list_table_content: The table shows titles, available languages, editorial status, and linked categories. Clicking the title also lets you copy the article shortcode.
posts_tour_form_translations_content: These tabs help you manage each language variant. Write the source language first, save it, then return here to adapt the other locales without losing the editorial direction.
posts_tour_form_editor_content: This column groups the title, slug, excerpt, and rich content for the active locale.
posts_tour_form_sidebar_content: The right column gathers status, featured image, categories, and SEO. This is where you truly finalize the article.
posts_tour_list_empty_content: Create your first source article here, then add its excerpt, category, featured image, and SEO. Translations come next once the main version is properly framed.
posts_tour_list_ready_next_content: Open an existing article now to review its source content, then complete the excerpt, featured image, categories, and finally the translations.
posts_tour_form_fields_content: Enter the title, slug, excerpt, and content of the article here. Build the source version first, then adapt each translation with the same editorial angle.
posts_tour_form_status_content: Use this block to keep the article as a draft or publish it. The right sequence is to finish the content, image, category, and SEO before going live.
posts_tour_form_media_content: Choose the main visual of the article here. Use the media library so the image fits the topic and stays consistent with the excerpt shown in lists.
posts_tour_form_taxonomies_content: Assign the article to one or more categories to organize the blog and make filtering easier. Do it before publishing to avoid orphaned content.
posts_tour_form_seo_content: Complete the SEO title and meta description for the active language after the article angle is finalized. This keeps the search snippet aligned with the real content.
posts_tour_form_create_next_content: Save the source article first, then complete the image, category, and SEO fields if needed. You will be able to open the other languages from the tabs afterwards.
posts_tour_form_edit_next_content: Save your changes, review the excerpt, featured image, and SEO, then finish the remaining translations. Publish only when the active version is complete.
posts_help_intro: Write the source post first, attach it to its category, add the featured image and SEO, then open translations once the base version is validated.
posts_help_step_source: Start with the source post, including title, excerpt, and main content.
posts_help_step_taxonomy: Then add at least one category and a featured image to keep the blog readable.
posts_help_step_translations: Finish with the other locales and publish only when the active version is really ready.
posts_empty_text: Create a first post to start the blog. Think about its category, featured image, and SEO before publishing.
